additional_changes_made_in_scope: Part A (X-Airlock-Mutations header) DONE — default-on, allowlist-aware/byte-bounded via mutations_header, gated on shared get_transparency_config() call; served-headers block left intact.

Part B (X-Airlock-Explain body envelope) DONE (not flagged) — empirical investigation: litellm ModelResponse uses pydantic model_config extra='allow', so a plain attribute (response.airlock = {...}) serializes into both model_dump() and model_dump_json() (the client-visible body). Implemented async_post_call_success_hook (non-streaming only; streaming iterator hook intentionally NOT implemented per Decision 7). Opt-in read from data['proxy_server_request']['headers'] case-insensitively vs cfg.explain_body_optin_header; falsy/absent value = byte-identical no-op (CC-T5). Test asserts against model_dump()/model_dump_json() so green proves client-visible serialization.
